title: Saved, named practice loops
description: Let users save a dragged loop region as a named item (e.g. "m. 12-16 shift", "solo bars 33-40", "tricky entrance at m. 25") that persists per piece and per recording in the .ovb bundle and IndexedDB, with a picker list and quick recall (e.g. number keys 1-9), so teachers and players stop re-dragging the same trouble spots every session.

title: Set loop points live from the keyboard
description: Add A/B-style loop marker keys (e.g. tap [ at the start and ] at the end of a lick) that set the loop region during playback, instead of stopping to drag across notes with the mouse.

title: Speed trainer / tempo ladder for loops
description: Add an optional loop mode that starts at a chosen speed (e.g. 60%) and raises playback by a configurable percent every N clean repetitions until it hits the target, automating the classic slow-practice ladder every teacher teaches manually.

title: Extend time-stretch below 50%, down to 25%
description: Lower the playback speed floor from 50% to 25% for both synth and recordings (degrading audio quality gracefully), since beginners, string students, and transcribers picking apart fast runs routinely need 25-40%.

title: Count-in, pre-roll, and configurable gap for loops
description: Make the count-in fire before the loop region (not just from bar 1) and honor the current speed; optionally play one bar of the recording or a sync-map-derived click as pre-roll, and allow a 1-3 second silence plus count-in between loop repeats so players can reset instead of getting dumped in cold.

title: One-key A/B toggle between synth and recording
description: Add a single hotkey and button that instantly swaps playback between the synced recording and the synth at the same musical position, both a core listening exercise in lessons and the fastest way to proof a transcription against the source.

title: Loop by bar numbers
description: Add a small input where users can type a range like "33-40" and hit enter to set the loop, because tab books and forum posts always reference bar numbers.

title: Snap waveform loops to synced bar boundaries
description: When a recording has a sync map, snap drag-to-loop endpoints on the waveform to bar boundaries (with a modifier key to override) so loops stop starting mid-beat.

title: Keyboard speed nudges
description: Bind minus/plus (or comma/period) to step playback speed down/up in 5% increments so users can creep a passage up to tempo without reaching for a slider.

title: One-click part balance presets (backing-track and hear-my-part modes)
description: Layer simple presets over the mute/solo matrix: a backing-track mode that mutes or ducks the user's own part while keeping loop and metronome, and a dropdown balance preset for SATB pieces (my part loud, others soft, or my part silent for testing).

title: Sync-map-driven metronome over the recording
description: Use the sync map to run the click along with the real recording, including through tempo drift, which also instantly exposes bars where sync points are off.

title: Record yourself with the microphone
description: Add a record button that captures the user via getUserMedia (over a loop, or while the score/accompaniment plays) into the recordings panel as just another recording on the piece, so self-review and teacher review use the exact same waveform and sync tools that already exist.

title: Side-by-side comparison of two recordings
description: Once two recordings exist on a piece (reference and student/self take), allow playing them in alternation or overlaid with a balance slider at matched speed, which is how teachers and players actually diagnose timing, tone, and phrasing drift.

title: Pitch-shift recordings by semitones and cents
description: Add a transpose control on recording playback (plus/minus semitones with fine cents) so Eb-tuned records, A=442 orchestras, capo'd guitars, and out-of-tune sources line up with the notation without retuning.

title: Streaming decode for long recordings
description: Decode and compute waveform peaks incrementally so a 90-minute rehearsal WAV opens without freezing the tab or exhausting memory.

title: Keyboard-nudgeable sync markers with precise timestamps
description: Give each per-bar sync marker focus and arrow-key nudging (e.g. 10ms with arrows, coarser/finer with modifiers), an announced and editable numeric timestamp, and optional looping playback around the marker while tuning, replacing the current drag-only interaction that is too coarse on a zoomed-out waveform and unusable without a pointer.

title: Drop-a-sync-point hotkey during playback
description: Add a key that plants a sync marker at the current bar while the recording plays, so users can refine auto-sync in one real-time pass instead of dragging markers afterward.

title: Per-bar sync confidence coloring
description: Tint auto-sync bar markers by onset-detection confidence (green/amber/red) so users know which bars to verify by ear instead of auditing the whole piece.

title: Undo history covering sync-map edits
description: Extend Cmd+Z to cover dragging sync markers, tap-along sync, and auto-sync runs, because destroying a hand-tuned sync map with one accidental drag is the scariest unrecoverable action in the app today.

title: Confirm-or-undo toast after auto-sync
description: After one-click auto-sync replaces an existing sync map, show a toast with an Undo button and a before/after marker count instead of silently overwriting the previous map.

title: Dotted notes and tuplets in the editor
description: Add a '.' key for augmentation dots and a tuplet mode (e.g. Ctrl+3 for triplets) to the duration vocabulary, since real repertoire (jigs, swing tunes) is unenterable without them and they slot cleanly into the existing 1/2/4/8/6/3 scheme.

title: Direct accidental entry keys
description: Add '+'/'-' (or similar) keys to sharpen and flatten the selected note chromatically with correct respelling, complementing the existing 'j' enharmonic toggle, since transpose-plus-respell is slow for chromatic passages.

title: Chord and multi-voice entry
description: Support adding an interval to the selected note (e.g. Shift+a-g) and a voice-2 layer per staff, since almost nothing arrangers work on is strictly monophonic.

title: Repeat-previous-bar key
description: Add a single keystroke that copies the previous measure into the current one, a huge time saver for vamps, riffs, and drum-adjacent parts.

title: Time and key signature changes mid-score, plus pickup bars
description: Let users insert meter and key changes at any barline in the editor, plus a pickup (anacrusis) bar option when creating a new score, because real tunes have them.

title: Click-drag range selection in the editor
description: Support shift-click and click-drag to select a run of notes so that delete, transpose, and copy/paste operate on ranges instead of forcing one-note-at-a-time editing.

title: Copy, cut, and paste for note ranges
description: Implement Cmd+C/X/V on range selections with paste-at-cursor semantics and a paste-preview ghost, the single biggest editing-speed multiplier missing from the editor.

title: Note entry audio feedback
description: Play the note through the existing synth whenever a pitch key or arrow transpose changes a note, with a mute toggle, so users can edit by ear the way every score editor allows.

title: Fingering and annotation layer in the editor
description: Support adding fingering numbers, circled string numbers, and free-text markings above notes (stored in the score and exported in MusicXML), because a score without the teacher's markings is only half the lesson.

title: Insert-mode caret indicator
description: When 'i' toggles insert mode, render a blinking caret between notes and a visible mode badge, because moded editing without a mode indicator is where users type garbage into their score.

title: Contextual hint bar in edit mode
description: Show a slim status strip under the score while editing that displays the current selection (pitch, duration, voice, bar) and the most relevant next-key hints, like a DAW's context-sensitive help line.

title: Visible undo/redo buttons with action labels
description: Add undo/redo toolbar buttons whose tooltips name the action being undone (e.g. "Undo: change D4 to eighth note"), backed by labeled entries in the existing undo stack.

title: Scope editor key handling off the global window
description: Move the window keydown listeners onto a focused score container with role and tabIndex, so typing 'a' in a text field or filename input doesn't rewrite the score.

title: Escape key consistently exits states
description: Make Esc uniformly clear the note selection, cancel an in-progress drag-loop, close overlays, and exit edit mode (with a confirm if there are unsaved structural edits), in that priority order.

title: Whole-score transposition
description: Add a transpose control (up/down by semitone or pick a key, with common instrument presets like Bb and Eb) that changes notation, playback, and export, for singers who need a different key and ensemble teachers accommodating transposing instruments.

title: Lyrics rendered under the notes and highlighted during playback
description: Display MusicXML lyrics, including multiple verses, and highlight the current syllable as the piece plays so singers can follow words, not just noteheads.

title: Section labels and jump navigation
description: Build a section list from rehearsal marks, double bars, and repeats (and let users mark their own: Intro, A, B, Bridge), shown as clickable chips or a sidebar with keyboard jumping, so "go to the bridge" takes one action instead of scrolling and hunting.

title: Jump to measure or rehearsal mark
description: Add a small go-to box that accepts a bar number or rehearsal letter and moves the cursor there, since rehearsals start "at letter B", not by scrolling.

title: Remember per-piece practice state
description: On reopening a piece, restore the last speed, active loop, selected part/recording, and playhead position from IndexedDB, so tonight's session (or a student reopening a link) picks up exactly where the last one ended.

title: Setlist / folder organization for pieces
description: Replace the flat session-persistence model with named collections (e.g. "Tuesday 4pm - Maya", "Spring recital") in IndexedDB, since a private teacher juggling 30 students needs per-student piece lists on day one.

title: Assignment notes attached to the score
description: Add a simple per-piece text panel (stored in the .ovb) where a teacher can write "this week: loop B section at 70%, hands together Friday" so the bundle itself carries the lesson plan, not a separate email.

title: First-run guided tour with a bundled demo piece
description: Ship a public-domain demo score plus a short dismissible coach-marks tour (drag to loop, slow it down, open the recording panel, enter edit mode) written in plain language for non-technical users, because features nobody discovers might as well not exist.

title: Keyboard shortcut cheat sheet overlay
description: Add a '?' key overlay (plus a toolbar help button) listing all playback and editor shortcuts grouped by task, since the a-g/1-8/i/x/t/j vocabulary is powerful but currently only documented in the README.

title: Autosave indicator and session-restore banner
description: Show a subtle "saved to this device" status with timestamp when IndexedDB persistence fires, a dismissible "Restored your last session" banner on load, and a warning before closing with unexported edits, so users trust the app with real work.

title: Selection and loop region visual polish
description: Give the selected note a high-contrast halo and the loop region a tinted band across the staff with draggable end handles, so state is readable at a glance instead of relying on alphaTab's default subtle highlight.

title: Responsive, touch-friendly layout for phones and tablets
description: Make the app and the embedded player reflow below 768px (collapsible toolbar, single column, notation reflow) with touch-sized transport, loop handles, and drag-to-loop, because students practice from links on phones and music stands hold tablets, and the PWA already works offline.

title: 44px minimum touch targets
description: Raise the 16-26px-wide track and sync-marker controls to at least 44x44px hit areas (padding can do it without changing visuals) per Apple HIG and WCAG 2.5.8.

title: Fix 100vh on mobile browsers
description: Replace min-height: 100vh with 100dvh (with fallback) so the transport bar isn't hidden behind Safari and Chrome mobile browser chrome.

title: Pinch and double-tap zoom on the waveform
description: Add pinch-to-zoom and double-tap-to-zoom gesture handling on the recording canvas, since the current +/- buttons are the only zoom path and are tiny on touch.

title: Distraction-free music-stand mode with wake lock
description: Add a fullscreen layout toggle that hides the editor and panels, enlarges transport/loop/tempo into big hit targets readable from music-stand distance, and uses the Screen Wake Lock API so a device on a stand doesn't sleep mid-practice.

title: Dark and high-contrast themes
description: Add a proper dark theme for the whole app (score background included) and honor prefers-color-scheme and prefers-contrast, verifying the 11-13px gray-on-gray labels reach 4.5:1, for night practice, low-vision users, and glare on outdoor gigs.

title: Larger type and user font scaling
description: Convert the hardcoded 11-13px font sizes to rem so browser text-size settings and iOS Dynamic Type actually enlarge labels, and bump the floor to a 14px equivalent.

title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion
description: Gate the follow-recording cursor's smooth scrolling and any animated transitions behind a prefers-reduced-motion media query, offering discrete page-style jumps instead.

title: Accessible names and tooltips on every control
description: Do a pass over App.tsx and RecordingPanel.tsx adding aria-labels, visible text, and title tooltips (including the keyboard shortcut in the tooltip) to all buttons, sliders, and toggles (zoom, clear loop, per-track mute/solo, sync marker handles), so a screen reader announces more than "button" and sighted users can learn the controls.

title: Visible focus indicators
description: Add a high-contrast :focus-visible outline style for buttons, checkboxes, sliders, and the score surface, since right now keyboard users have no idea where they are.

title: ARIA live region for playback and mode state
description: Announce play/pause, loop set/cleared, speed changes, edit-mode entry, and tap-sync progress via a polite live region so non-visual users get feedback for every action.

title: Slider semantics on the tempo control
description: Ensure the speed control is a real input[type=range] with aria-valuetext like "75 percent speed" and Home/End/PageUp support, not just a percentage number nearby.

title: Keyboard alternative for waveform loop and seek
description: Make the recording waveform canvas focusable with arrow-key seeking and Shift+arrows to set loop in/out points, since drag-to-loop on a canvas is unusable without a pointer.

title: Foot-pedal, media-key, and Web MIDI transport control
description: Map Space/Enter, Media Session API actions, Bluetooth page-turner PageUp/PageDown, and Web MIDI footswitches to configurable actions (play/pause, loop restart, speed up/down) so hands-on-instrument players never take hands off the guitar.

title: Automated a11y regression testing
description: Add axe-core checks and a keyboard-only Playwright smoke test (tab through transport, set a loop, change speed) to CI so accessibility fixes don't quietly rot.

title: Deep practice links with preset loop, speed, and part
description: Support URL params and fragments (e.g. ?bar=12&loop=12-16&speed=0.75 plus active recording and part balance) on both the app and the embedded player, so a teacher can paste a link into any LMS or text message and the student lands directly in the assigned drill with zero setup.

title: Copy-embed-code button in the export flow
description: After exporting a .ovb, show a ready-to-paste script-plus-div snippet with the bundle URL slot highlighted, closing the loop from authoring to publishing.

title: Graceful embed error states
description: Replace silent failures with clear in-player messages for 404 bundles, CORS blocks, and unsupported audio codecs, since broken first embeds kill publisher adoption.

title: Embed player lazy-loading and size budget
description: Split the embed bundle so notation, synth, and time-stretch code load on demand, publishing a documented kB budget in CI, because page-weight-sensitive publishers will benchmark against a YouTube iframe.

title: Accessible embed defaults
description: Give the embedded player an iframe title, a documented aria-label option, keyboard operability out of the box, and a JS API event for state changes so host pages can build accessible wrappers.

title: Hosted demo site with one-click 'Try it' bundles
description: Deploy the web app to a public URL with three preloaded example pieces so evaluators can experience score-plus-recording sync in under 30 seconds without cloning the repo.

title: Static-hosting cookbook in docs
description: Write step-by-step guides for hosting .ovb bundles on GitHub Pages, Netlify, and S3, including the CORS and content-type headers that will otherwise be everyone's first support issue.

title: Import from a URL, not just file picker
description: Let the web app open MusicXML, Guitar Pro, and .ovb files directly from a pasted URL so tutorials and issue reports can reference reproducible remote files, and evaluation requires zero downloads.

title: MusicXML round-trip fidelity and import compatibility test suite
description: Preserve dynamics, articulations, slurs, cross-voice ties, and directions on import and re-export, backed by an import compatibility matrix and automated round-trip tests against real-world MusicXML from MuseScore, Guitar Pro, and Dorico, with published results, since losing markings makes exports unusable for paid arranging work and publishers need to know their catalogs import cleanly.

title: Print and PDF export with real page layout
description: Add a print stylesheet and PDF export of the rendered notation with proper page breaks, measure numbers, title/composer headers, part names, and annotations, because lessons, choirs, and paying clients still need paper and the score is currently trapped in the browser.

title: Shareable .ovb gallery repo with submission workflow
description: Create an openvoicing-examples GitHub repo with a PR-based submission process for public-domain pieces, seeding both content for demos and a first contribution ritual for the community.

title: On-screen loop repeat counter
description: Show a small counter of how many times the current loop has repeated, with a reset button, so players can honestly track their "ten clean passes" rule.

title: Global spacebar play/pause
description: Make spacebar toggle playback no matter what has focus (and stop it from scrolling the page), so a player taking hands off the instrument can slap one key.

title: One-key half-speed toggle
description: A single hotkey that flips between 100% and the last-used slow speed, like stomping a pedal, for quickly re-checking a passage at tempo.

title: Big visual count-in
description: Show large countdown numbers (4, 3, 2, 1) on screen during the count-in with a distinct click sound, so young students know exactly when to come in.

title: Voice-appropriate playback sounds
description: Add a choir "ah" and a decent piano sound as playback options, because the default synth tone confuses young singers trying to match pitch.

title: MIDI keyboard step entry
description: Accept Web MIDI input so users can set the duration with number keys and play pitches (including chords) on a keyboard, the fastest entry method in every other notation app.

title: Screen-reader access to the score itself
description: Expose the alphaTab rendering with a parallel accessible layer (per-measure list with note names and durations, current selection announced), since a canvas score is a black box to VoiceOver.

title: Locked student/assignment mode
description: Add a read-only practice preset for share links and the embed JS API (locked loop region, capped speed, hidden editor/track-matrix/sync tools, showing only play, slow down, loop, and my-part volume) so a nine year old, or a school's LMS embed, is constrained to exactly the drill the teacher intended.

title: One-click publish to a free static host
description: Add a publish button that uploads the .ovb and returns a shareable player URL (GitHub Pages, Netlify drop, or similar), since teachers cannot run servers and hosting a file is the hardest step today.

title: Publish @openvoicing/embed to npm and a CDN
description: Ship the embed script as a versioned npm package with unpkg/jsDelivr URLs so bloggers can copy-paste a pinned script tag instead of hosting openvoicing-embed.js themselves.

title: TypeScript typings and typed event docs for the JS API
description: Ship .d.ts files and a generated API reference page covering create(), events, seek, setSpeed, and loop so tool builders can integrate without reading source.

title: Bundle format spec versioning and conformance fixtures
description: Version docs/bundle-format.md formally and publish a directory of valid and invalid .ovb fixtures so third-party implementers can test against the spec, which is the whole open-format bet.

title: CLI for creating and validating bundles
description: Ship an npx openvoicing CLI that packs a MusicXML file plus audio into a .ovb and validates existing bundles, enabling CI pipelines and batch conversion of existing tab libraries.

title: CONTRIBUTING.md with a 15-minute first-PR path
description: Document dev setup, an architecture map, and a curated 'good first issue' list per package so drive-by contributors convert instead of bouncing off the monorepo.
